entries.release_conflict_waiver_rationale: nist-tomap-010 (block_type_map: Caption → caption) sets type=caption as the default for ALL Caption elements. This rule is more specific: when the Caption's text equals 'Table of Contents' (case-insensitive), the element is the TOC heading, not a generic caption — type must be section_heading. The waiver acknowledges this intentional override of a block_type_map default by a more specific text_classifier_rule.

entries.checklist_item: extract_tables should populate per-cell bbox in raw.rows[*].cells[*].bbox so downstream bbox refinement can shrink an over-broad table.bbox to the union of its cell bboxes.

core_issue_spec.b5_dependency: B.5 cannot author a working applier rule_kind=bbox_refinement_rule until Rust exposes cell bboxes OR a separate table_postprocessor is built to coordinate classify_blocks + extract_tables.
core_issue_spec.defect_description: pdf_oxide.extract_tables emits row_count/col_count and a coarse table.bbox but does NOT expose per-cell bboxes in the raw output. On NIST 800-53r5 the coarse bbox is over-broad (occupies most of the page horizontally). With cell bboxes available, a deterministic post-processor could shrink table.bbox to union(cell.bbox). Today this signal is unavailable; the workaround would involve coordinating classify_blocks (text positions) with extract_tables, which belongs in a shared table_postprocessor layer rather than the ledger.

spot_check_findings.alternative_signal_available: doc.classify_blocks() within the table bbox returns ~24 text blocks per page (header row + body rows). Each text block has a real bbox. A post-processor could union those text-block bboxes to compute the tight table bbox WITHOUT a Rust change — but it would require classify_blocks + extract_tables coordination, which is naturally a table_postprocessor concern.
spot_check_findings.conclusion: 27 candidates route to pdf_oxide_core (core_component=table_extractor) for cell-bbox-exposure fix; B.5 also needs a downstream bbox-shrink rule once cell bboxes are available.
spot_check_findings.raw_table_bbox_observation: All three tables emit page-space bbox starting at x≈89.76, w=432.0 (occupies most of page horizontally), with row_count/col_count populated. rows[i] is a dict, NOT a list-of-cells with bboxes.

candidates_that_could_disprove: A table where the raw bbox correctly equals the cell-union (i.e., NOT over-broad). Such tables should not trigger the post-processor rule.
A non-NIST table fixture where cell bboxes are already exposed — would prove the gap is NIST-layout-specific, not core. Spot-check on a second PDF in B.4.1.

evidence_and_intent.deterministic_rule: extract_tables should expose per-cell bbox in the raw output (e.g., raw.rows[i].cells[j].bbox). With cell bboxes available, a post-processor rule (preset OR core invariant) can set table.bbox = union(cell.bbox) when the raw bbox is materially larger than the union (defining 'materially larger' as a tolerance the rule owns).
evidence_and_intent.lives_in: pdf_oxide_core (Rust src/tables/) — the cell-bbox-exposure gap is a core API completeness issue, not a NIST-specific transform.
evidence_and_intent.observed_pattern: All 27 NIST over-broad tables share the same shape: pdf_oxide.extract_tables emits a table.bbox covering most of the page, rows[i] are dicts (not lists of cells with bboxes), and no per-cell bbox field is exposed in the raw output. Spot-checked p19/p467/p490: each has row_count/col_count populated but `cells with bbox: 0`.

guards.counterexample_rationale_exempt: core_pending: rule is not active until Rust patch lands. Counterexample requirement applies to ledger-applied rules; this entry is a core_issue_spec marker.

entries.lessons_for_second_pass_agent: Future bbox_over_broad classifications should compute union(cell.bbox) before reporting over_broad. If the cells span the same area as the raw bbox, the table is NOT over-broad — it just covers a large region. Visual proportion to page size is not a reliable signal.
entries.rejection_rationale: B.5 deep spot-check on p19/p467/p490/p456 revealed that the raw table.bbox IS ALREADY EQUAL to union(cell.bbox) with 0.00pt drift across all 4 samples. The 104/172/140/172 cells in those tables each carry a populated bbox (accessed via rows[i].cells[j].bbox, NOT via the top-level cells field which doesn't exist). The second-pass agent's bbox_error_class='over_broad' label was a false positive — based on visual inspection ('table covers most of the page') rather than data inspection. No Rust patch is needed for these 27 candidates. They reclassify to positive_fixture.
